essence: Builds AI‑driven automation systems that control machines, robotics, and production workflows with adaptive, real‑time intelligence.

identity.title: AI Automation Engineer
identity.summary: Designs and deploys automation systems enhanced by machine learning, enabling equipment, robots, and production lines to make intelligent, real‑time decisions.

salary.notes: Engineers in aerospace, robotics, or fully autonomous smart‑factory environments can exceed £150k-£200k+.

qualifications: Experience in automation engineering, robotics, or AI‑driven control systems
Knowledge of PLCs, sensors, and industrial automation platforms
Skills in machine learning, optimisation, and real‑time decision systems
Understanding of CNC, robotics, and Industry 4.0 workflows

lifestyle.description: AI automation engineers work in aerospace, robotics, automotive, and Industry 4.0 environments. They build intelligent automation systems that adapt to real‑time conditions, optimise performance, and support autonomous manufacturing. The role suits individuals who enjoy robotics, AI, and high‑tech engineering.

habits: Designs AI‑enhanced automation and control systems
Integrates robotics, sensors, and machine‑learning models
Collaborates with reliability, production, and AI teams
Ensures safe, efficient, autonomous operation of equipment
